content_summary: Describes the generation of the Tabula Muris Senis atlas: scRNA-seq on more than 350000 cells from male and female C57BL/6JN mice across 23 tissues at six age groups (1, 3, 18, 21, 24, and 30 months) using FACS and droplet methods.

location: Main text - Cell annotation and quality control (page 590, para 2-3)
content_summary: Reports that 529823 total cells were sequenced, with 110824 FACS and 245389 droplet cells passing filtering. Describes use of Tabula Muris (3-month) as reference for semi-automated annotation, achieving >70% automated annotation with expert curation.

location: Main text - Batch correction and clustering (page 590, para 3)
content_summary: Describes application of bbknn batch-alignment algorithm and Louvain graph-based clustering to assess consistency of cell annotations across the organism, showing that B cells and endothelial cells cluster by type irrespective of tissue or method.

location: Main text - Ageing-related changes in gene expression (page 590, para 4)
content_summary: Reports that the Tabula Muris Senis enables discovery of ageing-related changes in specific cell types, resolving whether bulk expression changes are due to changes in cell numbers or per-cell expression. Global analysis with bulk RNA-seq from tissues shows both mechanisms contribute.

Reports that the fraction of cells expressing Cdkn2a and its protein product p16 more than doubled in older mice compared to younger mice by both FACS and droplet methods. Notes that 30-month-old mice show a smaller fraction of p16-expressing cells than 24-month-old mice.

Using a list of previously characterized senescence markers, plots the fraction of cells expressing each marker across all age groups. Cdkn2a has the highest correlation with ageing; other genes with positive correlation include E2f2, Lmnb1, Tnf, and Itgax. Sirt family genes decrease with age.

Reports pronounced changes in bladder cell-type composition with age: the mesenchymal compartment decreases by a factor of three, while the urothelial compartment increases. Differential gene expression shows stromal genes downregulated and epithelial genes upregulated.

Reports decline of the endothelial population in bladder suggesting lower organ vascularization. Increase in leukocyte population indicates inflammatory tissue microenvironment. Overexpression of Lgals3, Igfbp2, and Ly6d and immune-response genes in old leukocytes.

Reports decrease in relative abundance of mesangial cells, capillary endothelial cells, loop of Henle ascending limb epithelial cells, and loop of Henle thick ascending limb epithelial cells with age. Reduction of Egf and Atp1a1 expression suggests impaired glomerular filtration rate.

Reports that expression of Umod (encoding uromodulin) decreases across the tissue with age due to reduction in the proportion of ascending limb and thick ascending limb epithelial cells. As with Atp1a1, Umod expression increases in the cell type that becomes less abundant.

Reports that the proportion of T cells decreases while the relative amount of plasma cells increases with age in the spleen. Supported by upregulation of B cell and plasma cell markers Cd79a and Jchain and downregulation of Cd3d.

location: Main text - Mammary gland T cell decline (page 592)
content_summary: Reports a decline of the T cell population in the mammary gland with age, noting that age-related decline of T cell populations is associated with increased risk of infectious disease and cancer.

location: Main text - AP1 transcription factors in mammary gland (page 592-593)
content_summary: Reports that AP1 transcription factors (Junb, Jund, and Fos) are upregulated with age in mammary gland, consistent with increased expression of this gene family accompanying normal mammary gland involution.

Reports age-related decrease in hepatocyte proportion (supported by reduction of albumin Alb expression), increased immune signature (overexpression of H2-Aa, H2-Ab1, H2-D1, H2-Eb1, Cd74, Lyz2), and identification of Il1b as showing markedly different expression with age.

location: Main text - Liver Kupffer cells and Il1b (page 593)
content_summary: Reports that the number of cells expressing both Clec4f and Il1b increased with age. In situ RNA staining confirmed Clec4f-positive Kupffer cell numbers do not change, but co-staining reveals Il1b increases in Kupffer cells with age.

location: Main text - Liver sinusoidal endothelial cells (LSECs) (page 593)
content_summary: Reports increased Mrc1 expression in Kupffer cells while overall Mrc1 in endothelial cells decreased with age. In situ staining for Mrc1 alongside LSEC marker Pecam1 showed the number of Mrc1-expressing LSECs increased with age, though Mrc1 expression per cell did not increase.

location: Main text - Genomic instability / mutation analysis (page 593)
content_summary: Using the Genome Analysis ToolKit to identify single-nucleotide polymorphisms across all FACS samples, reports an age-related increase in the number of mutations across all organs, with tongue and bladder most affected. Controlled for sequencing coverage and verified against ERCC spike-in controls.

location: Main text - Immune clonal analysis - B cells (page 593)
content_summary: Computationally reconstructed B cell receptor sequences from FACS data. Reports 6050 B cells with assembled receptors. The proportion of cells belonging to a clonal family increased from 9% at 3 months to 20% at 18 months and doubled from 18 to 24 months (~38%).

location: Main text - Immune clonal analysis - T cells (page 593)
content_summary: Computationally reconstructed T cell receptor sequences from FACS data using TraCeR. Reports 6000 T cells with assembled receptors. Clonotype assignment shows proportion of cells part of a clone increased from ~3% at 3 months to ~23% at 18 months and ~20% at 24 months.

Computed an overall diversity score (Shannon entropy of cluster assignment regressed against age) to identify cell types more susceptible to changes with age. Found significant changes in diversity for immune cells from brain and kidney.

location: Main text - Brain microglia trajectory analysis (page 594)
content_summary: Trajectory analysis of brain myeloid microglial cells shows young microglia transition through an intermediate state. Old microglia (clusters 10, 12, 14) upregulate MHC class I genes (H2-D1, H2-K1, B2m) and genes associated with degenerative disease (Fth1). Differential expression resembles Alzheimer's disease-specific microglial signature.

Reports two kidney macrophage clusters with age-dependent composition. Cluster 10 is enriched for M2-macrophage genes (Il10, H2-Eb1, H2-Ab1, H2-Aq, Cd74, Cxcl16, Hexb, Cd81, Clqb, Cd72), while cluster 13 resembles an M1-proinflammatory macrophage state (Hp, Itgal, Msrb1, Gngt2).

Describes mouse collection from NIA colony at Charles River, housing at VMU and VA Palo Alto. C57BL/6JN mice, male and virgin female, ages 1m through 30m. Tissue collection from 23 organs with organ-specific dissociation protocols.
Describes FACS sorting protocol using Smart-seq2 and library preparation using Tn5 in-house version. References protocols.io for further details.
Describes droplet capture using GemCode Single-Cell Instrument (10x Genomics) with 3-prime Gel Bead and Library V2 Kit. Target output of 5000 cells per sample.

location: Methods - In situ RNA hybridization and quantification
content_summary: Describes Advanced Cell Diagnostics RNAscope used for liver staining of Clec4f, Il1b, Pecam1, and Mrc1 probes to validate scRNA-seq findings in liver tissue at 3, 12, and 30 months.
Describes computational pipeline: bcl2fastq for de-multiplexing, STAR v.2.5.2b for FACS alignment, HTSEQ v.0.6.1p1 for gene counts, CellRanger v.2.0.1 for droplet de-multiplexing and alignment.

Describes use of Scanpy v.1.4.2 for combining gene count tables with metadata, quality filtering criteria (min 3 cells, 250 genes, 5000 reads for FACS, 2500 UMIs for droplet), normalization, PCA, neighbourhood graph construction, Louvain and Leiden clustering, and UMAP visualization.
Describes per-organ independent analysis combining all ages, using Tabula Muris 3-month data subset for automated annotation, followed by expert review. Step-by-step instructions available on GitHub.
Describes computation of relative proportion of each cell type per tissue and age using scipy.stats linear regression against age, with significance testing using two-sided Wald test.

Describes MAST linear model for differential expression with age as continuous variable, controlling for sex and technology, with FDR threshold of 0.01 and age coefficient threshold of 0.005 (~10% fold change).

location: Methods - Comparison between bulk and single-cell datasets
content_summary: Describes per-tissue differential gene analysis comparing FACS/droplet single-cell data with bulk RNA-seq, using Spearman correlation and Wilcoxon-Mann-Whitney test to assess whether log2 fold-changes in cell or read counts distinguish up- and downregulated bulk genes.
Describes use of TraCeR v.0.5 with dependencies (igblast, kallisto, Salmon, Trinity) and species Mmus to identify T cell clonal populations from FACS data.
Describes use of singlecell-ige v.eafb6d126cc2d6511faae3efbd442abd7c6dc8ef to identify B cell clonal populations from FACS data.

Describes use of samtools v.1.9 and GATK v.4.1.1.0 for mutation calling on FACS data, including index creation, read group assignment, and HaplotypeCaller. Results summarized per gene as mutation count per cell table.
Describes use of partition-based graph abstraction (PAGA) to reconstruct the ageing trajectory in brain microglial cells from FACS data.

Describes computation of Shannon entropy-based diversity score using raw FACS or droplet data, with clustering of every tissue-cell type for every mouse separately using 6 different configurations (3 resolutions x 2 methods). FDR correction for multiple comparisons with FDR < 0.3 threshold.

Explicitly states the dataset can be explored at tabula-muris-senis.ds.czbiohub.org, gene counts and metadata at figshare (doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.8273102.v2), Gene Expression Omnibus accession GSE132042, and raw data at AWS S3 bucket.
States all analysis code is available from GitHub at https://github.com/czbiohub/tabula-muris-senis.
